title: Panna cotta – bite by bite

seo.title: Panna cotta - bite by bite - Marinda Kook

html:
I always order the clean, smooth dessert and then I think it must be terribly difficult to make. Until I made it myself and realised it's really easy to make. Some people are afraid to work with gelatine and really you just need to read what's on the packet.

YOU'LL NEED:

  • 500 ml cream (don't skimp)
  • 15 ml gelatine (one sachet in the box)
  • 90 ml warm water
  • 200 ml castor sugar
  • 5 ml vanilla essence

This is for the top layer:

  • 1 Flake
  • 30 ml milk

NOW:

  1. pour the warm water into a cup and sprinkle the gelatine on top of the water. Set aside.
  2. heat the cream, castor sugar and vanilla essence until just before it boils, the sugar must be dissolved.
  3. stir the gelatine mixture in the cup to make sure the gelatine has dissolved
  4. now add the gelatine mixture to the warm cream and stir it through.
  5. spray containers of your choice with Spray and Cook and pour the cream mixture into the containers
  6. place in the fridge for at least 4 hours
  7. melt the Flake and milk in a container in the microwave for 1 minute. Stir it through.
  8. now you can decide to serve the panna cotta in its container – spread some of the chocolate mixture over it
  9. or you can loosen it with a knife and turn it out onto a plate. Now drizzle the chocolate mixture over it.

It is terribly rich and you should serve small portions. Be creative and serve berries on top, or melt strawberry jam in the microwave and spread it over your panna cotta.

Little effort for great joy.
